impact: data-stop bubble-matching semantics worth documenting. NOT addressed in this round — deferred to ISS-021 (data-click-arg contract) which will revisit dispatcher semantics as a coherent block.
resolution:
Documented data-stop bubble-matching semantics in the static/js/actions.js header doc, alongside the existing data-stop spec.

Key clarification: data-stop is read from the SAME element matched by closest('[data-<event>]'). If a child has its own data-click without data-stop, that child handler runs and the event bubbles past without ever inspecting the parent's data-stop. To "stop above" from inside, the data-stop must live on the child's data-click element (typical pattern), OR wrap the children in an outer data-click="noop" data-stop sink (the admin_users.html pattern from T-031).

Doc-only change, no behavior change. Originally deferred to ISS-021 (data-click-arg contract) but pulled forward as a standalone fix since the semantics are confusing enough to deserve their own callout regardless of when data-click-arg lands.
